headline: FCPT Appoints Michael Friedland to the Board of Directors
summary: MILL VALLEY, Calif., April 01, 2026--Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE:FCPT), a real estate investment trust primarily engaged in the ownership of high-quality, net-leased restaurant and retail properties ("FCPT" or the "Company"), announced today that Michael Friedland has been appointed to the Company’s Board of Directors (the "Board"), effective April 1, 2026.

headline: More private credit loans for sale than ever, but secondary demand appears tepid
summary: More private credit loans are up for sale than ever before on a nascent secondary market, driven by elevated investor redemptions in BDCs that are pushing credit managers to seek ways of boosting liquidity in a skittish market. However, the elevated inventory of loans for sale does not necessarily mean that secondary trading has increased significantly, market participants say. Instead, the supply of loans is outpacing demand for them, and aftermarket liquidity for private credit loans is thin.

Instead, the supply of loans is outpacing demand for them, and aftermarket liquidity for private credit loans is thin.

headline: What Banks Stand to Lose From the Private-Credit Mess
summary: As the start of their earnings season approaches, investors are scrutinizing banks for their exposure to nonbank financial firms, and in particular the private funds that have seen a surge of redemptions.  The KBW Nasdaq Bank Index is down around 6%, versus the S&P 500’s roughly 4.5% decline, so far this year.  There has been a surge in recent years of bank lending to nonbanks, or so-called non-depository financial institutions, or NDFIs.

headline: How The Syensqo (ENXTBR:SYENS) Investment Story Is Shifting As Analysts Turn More Cautious
summary: Syensqo’s central fair value estimate has been trimmed from €69.86 to €66.08, a shift that puts fresh focus on where analysts think the shares can realistically trade. At the same time, recent research shows a split view, with some firms cutting ratings and targets toward €54 to €57, while others, such as JPMorgan, still sit higher at €67.50.
